title: Create Admin Dashboard
description: Build a complete admin dashboard with elevated permissions including user management, role assignment, and audit logging.
instructions:
Extend the role-based system from Lesson 1 to build a full-featured Admin Dashboard for ShopFlow!

**Requirements:**

1. **User Management Endpoints:**
   - GET /api/admin/users - List all users with pagination (Admin only)
   - GET /api/admin/users/{id} - Get user details including roles
   - PUT /api/admin/users/{id}/status - Enable/disable user account
   - DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} - Soft-delete a user (mark inactive)

2. **Role Management UI Endpoints:**
   - GET /api/admin/roles - List all available roles
   - POST /api/admin/users/{id}/roles - Add role to user
   - DELETE /api/admin/users/{id}/roles/{role} - Remove role from user
   - GET /api/admin/users/{id}/permissions - Get effective permissions for user

3. **Audit Logging:**
   - Create AuditLog entity to track admin actions
   - Log all user/role modifications with: admin ID, action, target user, timestamp, IP
   - GET /api/admin/audit-logs - View audit trail (Admin only)

4. **Dashboard Statistics:**
   - GET /api/admin/stats - Return aggregated stats:
     * Total users by role
     * New users this week/month
     * Recent login activity
     * Pending role assignments

**Security Requirements:**
- All endpoints require Admin role
- Audit all administrative actions
- Prevent self-lockout (admin cannot disable their own account)
- Validate inputs to prevent injection attacks

**Edge Cases:**
- Handle non-existent user IDs gracefully
- Prevent removing the last Admin role from a user
- Log failed admin actions (attempted unauthorized access)

**Hint:** Use a service layer to encapsulate business logic and ensure audit logging happens consistently.

testCases.description: Should have Admin authorization on all admin endpoints

hints.text: Create a UserManagementService to encapsulate user CRUD operations and keep controllers thin.
Inject IHttpContextAccessor to get the current admin's ID for audit logging.
Use UserManager<ApplicationUser>.GetUsersInRoleAsync to get users by role for statistics.
For pagination, accept pageNumber and pageSize parameters and return a PaginatedResult<T>.
In the delete endpoint, check if user.Id == currentUserId and return BadRequest to prevent self-deletion.

commonMistakes.consequence: Security blind spots - you won't detect brute force or unauthorized access attempts.
commonMistakes.correction: Log both successful and failed admin actions, including the reason for failure.

commonMistakes.consequence: Data loss and broken referential integrity (orders, reviews linked to deleted users).
commonMistakes.correction: Add an IsActive flag and filter it in queries. Keep data for audit trails.

commonMistakes.mistake: Not checking for last admin before role removal
commonMistakes.consequence: Could lock out all admins from the system requiring database intervention.
commonMistakes.correction: Count admins before allowing role removal. If count == 1, prevent the operation.

commonMistakes.mistake: Exposing sensitive user data in list endpoints
commonMistakes.consequence: GDPR/privacy violations by exposing personal information unnecessarily.
commonMistakes.correction: Create DTOs that only expose necessary fields. Don't return password hashes, tokens, etc.
